text:
chool days, he drank, smoked, and was kind of wild.
He said he just liked having fun (He says) he’s absolutely not like that now, but it still bothers me a lot Of course, while dating me, he has never made my life hard or given me reason to worry - But this is making you worry right now - And making things hard for you I can really feel that he only has eyes for me and that he loves me very much, and now he really only works and only sees me ㅠㅜ He’s even trying to quit smoking, which he’s done since middle school, because of me, - He’s trying~ - Right, so he hasn’t quit, has he? (Chaotic chemistry) - Just trying - I try too (Cold) He says he thinks he fell in with the wrong crowd during his school days, and now he doesn’t even keep in touch with those friends anymore The past is the past, so I should try not to care, right? I want to stop caring too!! But even so, his past keeps coming to mind and making things hard for me When I tell my boyfriend that I’m upset about it, he always says, “I’ll reassure you even more,” comforts me, and always speaks kindly to me... Is there any way I can stop caring about it? - Unni, I always think this last part is important - It’s not “Should I break up with him?” - It’s “Is there a way I can stop caring?” - That part just feels so - painful to me, unni - You can really feel how much she loves him, right? - She must really love him
- Yes (x3) - What really surprised me was that - how did it even come up... - that he had one-night stands like that - for 3 to 4 months? - How did that even come up in conversation? - I seriously don’t understand that - I mean, even if he did! - Exactly! - At what moment exactly - did he end up saying that? - What were they even talking about? - Was he bragging or something? (Aha?) - Hey, just how far did you.. - “Until when did you live like trash?” - “How much did you live like trash?” - Ugh, seriously... (I’m not doing this) @$$$
- You have to, there’s PPL (Pulling herself back together) - “The past is the past, so I should try not to care, right?” - She says that, - but once he told her this, - how can she not care about it? - I think it would keep coming to mind no matter what, - and if he really loved her, - this would actually be hurtful to say, - so if he loved her, - he wouldn’t have brought this up at all @Something that never needed to be said or heard
- If he never brought it up, she never would’ve known - So I’m not saying, - “This guy is going to do it again,”

title: [SUB] 그외 골치 아픈 사연 다수 | 퀸즈나잇 [Queen’s Night] EP5🌙
text: t him along, saying he was a younger friend they knew As the gathering was wrapping up, we were talking about a movie I’d been waiting to see, and he said, “Oh! I want to see that movie too, do you want to go together?” “Sure!” So we met up separately, watched the movie, and ended up having dinner together But while we were on our way to eat, he said this “Actually, I don’t really know what this movie is about. I only wanted to see it because you said you wanted to, noona.” Up until then, I just said, “Oh~ I see.” But after that, we started seeing each other just the two of us Going to see cherry blossoms, taking walks, watching movies, taking walks again, and visiting good restaurants together And he said, “Noona, I think we really get along well.” From then on, I started seeing him a little differently too But this guy? He didn’t keep the contact going consistently Maybe replying to each other’s stories once every month or two? If there was something he wanted to do, he’d contact me, and that just kept repeating But that winter, something happened... We got swept up in the mood... Because of where we were, we didn’t go all the way - Then how far did you go? What I felt at that moment wasn’t excitement or butterflies but discomfort When it actually happened, I was so stunned that I couldn’t think of anything but on the way home I kept replaying it in my head and I started thinking, “Uh... what does he even think I am?” Even after that, whenever we met I thought, “Ah, I don’t have any feelings for him anymore,” but when we were together it was still fun and we still got along well And both of us stayed silent about what happened then Then one day, I heard that he had gotten a girlfriend At that moment, it felt like I’d been hit on the back of the head Of course, I didn’t really do anything myself to move the relationship forward either but I felt like chewed gum that had been spit out so I thought I had completely let go of my feelings for him After some time passed like that he contacted me again asking if we could meet I had heard that he had already broken up with his girlfriend and in the list of movies he sent me there was a movie I really, really wanted to see - It’s true! - It was because of that movie! - So I just decided to meet him When I saw him again it was a little awkward but he acted totally normal like we’d always been close, taking care of me and even giving me his jacket and he kept saying how much he’d thought about

text:
to the story Looking back now it was a ridiculous method, but at the time I think I wanted to believe it After two years of dating, we finally broke up early last year Even after we broke up he kept contacting me until July saying “I still like you” and “I even thought about marrying you” When I said I was getting off work with a coworker, he got jealous and said, “I can’t just say ‘Be careful and don’t get into an accident on your way home’ like I normally would,”
and then, “I’m jealous, I’m mad” - Why are you mad? (One day, “mad” sprouted in the story) - Unni, the way you say it is so perfectly blended He kept saying things like that Sometimes he’d say things like, “You looked happy,” and hearing that made me think maybe he still had lingering feelings, so I got shaken again Then one day, a wedding invitation was posted on the company bulletin board (Looking away) It was my ex-boyfriend’s wedding invitation But what was even more shocking was... the bride’s name was the exact name of the woman he had said was his ex-girlfriend A long time ago, during a fight, I had written that name down in my notes so I wouldn’t forget it - Why would you write that down in your notes?! So I was sure The wedding date was February 21 Since I found out about the wedding, he hasn’t contacted me even once But the even funnier thing is, you know that cookie status message? I checked his profile, and even now, right before the wedding, his status message was still “Cookie” (The status message he said he’d change once he got a new girlfriend) Honestly, I even wanted to go to the wedding and tell the bride everything... But I still have to keep seeing him at work, and now he’s basically a stranger, but I felt so wronged that I told a few people at work, and honestly, I still don’t understand it Up until June last year he was saying he still liked me, and even until July he was getting jealous and sending me his location to reassure me and then the same person who was talking to me with a smile in November was getting married in February... So what on earth was I to him? It’s not lingering feelings—I’m just so angry and upset that my sincere feelings were caught up in his lies (Un-com-fortable) - Unni, you’ve been through a lot - Through a lot... unni lol

Mackerel3 (teens) This is a story from a teenage friend 
Mackerel3 (teens) Hello, I’m a high school senior preparing for this year’s CSAT I’m writing in because I don’t know how I should act There used to be four people in our family, including my older sister and me, but my mother passed away two years ago after battling cancer for ten years Each of us went through a hard time But starting late last year, my dad was talking on the phone almost every night with someone in his room before bed I didn’t think he talked on the phone with acquaintances or family every single day like that, so I just kept wondering to myself, what is this? Later, I happened to see my dad’s call history, and there was a contact saved with the name “Flower Deer” That’s usually a word used to refer to a woman, right? So I became sure that there was someone new in his life After that, I only told my sister, and I looked through some of his KakaoTalk messages little by little, and there were even photos of the two of them traveling together The place they went together was somewhere our family used to go often And there were also hotel reservations for a trip to Da Nang in April My sister is already an adult, and I’ll be an adult soon too, and I think my dad has his own life as well But on the other hand, even though I’ll be an adult soon, I’m still a student preparing for entrance exams, and a senior in high school who can’t even retake the year because the curriculum is changing So at a time like this, I’m disappointed that he’s going on a trip to Da Nang Of course, my dad also cares a lot about my studies and college entrance exams But I keep thinking, “Did he really have to go this year?” And besides that, is it really possible to meet someone new after only about two years? I do think it could happen later on But forgetting someone you lived with for over 20 years in just two years and meeting someone new is still something I can’t understand I know my dad has his own life, but I keep thinking of my mom and I feel disappointed What should I do?

Our friend

(I don’t think there’s any need to jump to that conclusion) - And something else I’d like her to think about more is, - then exactly how much time - would have to pass before Dad could meet someone? - Would it be okay if he met someone a few years later?
- I think it’s this - Our friend is in a situation right now where - she desperately needs her dad - She’s a high school senior, still a minor, - and she hasn’t even gone to college yet, - so right now she’s someone who desperately needs - her parents’ attention, love, - and support so, so much, - and with her mother gone, - Dad is all she has, - so she wants Dad to give all of himself - completely to her, - and because it doesn’t feel like that’s happening, - I think that’s probably part of why she feels disappointed
- Because once this girl goes to college - and gets a boyfriend around her sophomore year, - she’ll totally support - her dad meeting someone new and dating - But it’s just not now - Right now, I’m not ready for it - Right now, I want him to give his love only to me - And I understand that feeling too
- Right, I understand it - But - the love a child can give - and the love from Dad’s partner, “Flower Deer,” - and the love from Dad’s partner, “Flower Deer,” - the care, love, and comfort she can give, - are very different (A category of comfort that’s bound to be different) - There had to be some place elsewhere - that could fill - that part of Dad’s life too - That... ah - I know that so well, @Feeling upset in a way that’s so understandable
- and I know this must be so hard for our friend, (They hope she’ll also think about her dad’s position) - So this is it - Right now, she has to - manage these feelings well
@For the sender’s sake That’s why I want to keep telling her this - I can’t really talk about this in detail either, (Jiyu has a relative who has experienced bereavement) - but the heart of the person left behind, - from the perspective of

We’re not even married yet, so should I just let him have all the fun he wants?” but then I go back to, “He’s not changing even now—will he really change after marriage?” What should I do? - You absolutely should not marry him~ - Stop right there~ @Satisfied
